hero.label: Made for Dex
hero.headline: I want to build Dex into the network operating system.
hero.subheadline: Kevin Sun solved his own problem by building Dex, and I've spent the last year solving other people's problems through outreach and relationship-building. Now I want to help thousands of professionals do what I've learned matters most: actually staying connected with the people who matter.

whyCompany.title: Dex solves a real, manual, frustrating problem
whyCompany.body: Kevin built Dex because managing relationships across LinkedIn, Gmail, and Facebook was tedious. I've lived this friction as a BDR: tracking hundreds of contacts, remembering context, following up manually. A personal CRM that centralizes and reminds you to stay in touch isn't a nice-to-have, it's essential for anyone building a real network.

whyCompany.title: Scaling a proven product with thousands of customers
whyCompany.body: Dex has 5 years of product-market fit and thousands of happy paying customers. The timing to hire a Founding Growth Lead means they're ready to accelerate from niche to mainstream. This is the inflection point where early-stage hustle meets real distribution leverage.

whyCompany.title: My BDR work is directly transferable here
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai, I acquired 15-20 paying clients through cold outreach and LinkedIn sequences. At NextAftar, I learned B2B fundraising and what makes professionals actually engage. For Dex, that means I understand both sides: the person trying to reach someone (BDR perspective) and the pain of managing those relationships (product perspective). I can speak the language of both users and buyers.

quote: I've spent a year helping other companies acquire customers. Now I want to help people actually keep the relationships they've built.
